pred_answer: README 说 `nvdlav1` 这个 release 是 NVDLA 的非可配置 “full-precision” 版本（repo_sources/nvdla/hw/README.md:15-16），固定为 2048 个 8-bit MAC，或 1024 个 16-bit fixed-/floating-point MAC（repo_sources/nvdla/hw/README.md:17），并且预期是稳定维护版（repo_sources/nvdla/hw/README.md:18-19）。

evidence.statement: README states nvdlav1 contains the non-configurable full-precision version and fixed MAC counts.
evidence.text:
## About this release

This release, in the `nvdlav1` branch, contains the non-configurable
"full-precision" version of NVDLA.  This non-configurable version is fixed
at 2048 8-bit MACs (or 1024 16-bit fixed- or floating-point MACs).  This
branch is expected to be a stable sustaining release; although bug fixes may
be added, new RTL feature improvements will not appear in this branch.

pred_answer: README 有说明：`nvdla/hw` 包含 RTL、C-model 和 testbench 代码；其中 `vmod/` 是 RTL model，含 NVDLA 的 Verilog 实现、库/单元模型和 RAM 行为模型；`syn/` 是 NVDLA 示例综合脚本；`verif/` 是用于基本 sanity validation 的 trace-player testbench，并含示例 traces；`tools` 是构建 RTL 以及运行仿真/综合等使用的工具。（repo_sources/nvdla/hw/README.md:33-47）

evidence.statement: README lists RTL, C-model, testbench and the vmod, syn, verif, tools, spec directories.
evidence.text:
## Directory Structure

This repository contains the RTL, C-model, and testbench code associated with the NVDLA hardware 
release.  In this repository, you will find:

  * vmod/ -- RTL model, including:
    * vmod/nvdla/ -- Verilog implementation of NVDLA
    * vmod/vlibs/ -- library and cell models
    * vmod/rams/ -- behavioral models of RAMs used by NVDLA
  * syn/ -- example synthesis scripts for NVDLA
  * perf/ -- performance estimator spreadsheet for NVDLA
  * verif/ -- trace-player testbench for basic sanity validation
    * verif/traces/ -- sample traces associated with various networks
  * tools -- tools used for building the RTL and running simulation/synthesis/etc.
  * spec -- RTL configuration option settings.

evidence.text:
## Building the NVDLA Hardware

See the [integrator's manual](http://nvdla.org/integration_guide.html) for more information on 
the setup and other build commands and options.  The basic build command to compile the design
and run a short sanity simulation is:

    bin/tmake

pred_answer: 是。SW README 明确说 KMD 是 Linux out-of-tree kernel module；DMA buffer 使用 DRM 和 GEM PRIME 进行分配和共享（repo_sources/nvdla/sw/README.md:17-23）。

evidence.statement: SW README says KMD is Linux out-of-tree module and uses DRM and GEM PRIME for DMA buffers.
evidence.text:
## Kernel Mode Driver

The kernel mode driver (KMD) is supported as a Linux out-of-tree kernel module.
It has been verified with Linux 4.13.3 on ARM64 and is expected to work
on other cpu architectures with little or no modification.
The driver uses DRM and GEM PRIME for DMA buffer allocation and sharing.

pred_answer: UMD 包含 runtime library，负责提供接口从 loadable 加载 network，并把它提交给 NVDLA KMD；所以更准确地说，不是“直接把 loadable 原样交给 KMD”，而是通过 UMD/runtime 从 loadable 加载网络后提交给 KMD。(repo_sources/nvdla/sw/README.md:27-31)

evidence.statement: SW README says UMD includes runtime library, loads network from loadable, and submits it to KMD.
evidence.text:
## User Mode Driver and test application

The user mode driver (UMD) includes runtime library. It provides interfaces to load
network from loadable and submit it to NVDLA KMD. For reference, this package also
includes test application demonstrating usage of runtime interfaces.

pred_answer: runtime environment 分成两块：user mode driver（UMD）和 kernel mode driver（KMD）（repo_sources/nvdla/doc/doc/sw/runtime_environment.rst:11-16）。

evidence.statement: Runtime documentation says runtime environment consists of UMD and KMD.
evidence.text:
The runtime envionment includes software to run a compiled neural network on compatible :term:`NVDLA` hardware. It consists of 2 parts:

* :ref:`user_mode_driver` - This is the main interface to the application. As detailed in the :ref:`compiler_library`, after parsing and compiling the neural network layer by layer, the compiled output is stored in a file format called :term:`NVDLA Loadable`. User mode runtime driver loads this loadable and submits inference jobs to the :ref:`kernel_mode_driver`.

* :ref:`kernel_mode_driver` - Consists of kernel mode driver and engine scheduler that does the work of scheduling the compiled network on :term:`NVDLA` and programming the :term:`NVDLA` registers to configure each functional block.

pred_answer: runtime_environment.rst 说，NVDLA Loadable 中各 layer 通过 dependency graph 连接；这个 dependency graph 是给 engine scheduler（引擎调度器）用来调度 layers 的。见 repo_sources/nvdla/doc/doc/sw/runtime_environment.rst:17-19。

evidence.statement: Runtime documentation says layers are linked through dependency graph, used by engine scheduler for scheduling layers.
evidence.text:
The runtime environment uses the stored representation of the network saved as :term:`NVDLA Loadable` image. From point of view of the :term:`NVDLA Loadable`, each compiled "layer" in software is loadable on a functional block in the :term:`NVDLA` implementation. Each layer includes information about its dependencies on other layers, the buffers that it uses for inputs and outputs in memory, and the specific configuration of each functional block used for its execution. Layers are linked together through a dependency graph, which the engine scheduler uses for scheduling layers. The format of an :term:`NVDLA Loadable` is standardized across compiler implementations and UMD implementations. All implementations that comply with the :term:`NVDLA` standard should be able to at least interpret any :term:`NVDLA Loadable` image, even if the implementation may not have some features that are required to run inferencing using that loadable image.

Both the :ref:`user_mode_driver` stack and the :ref:`kernel_mode_driver` stack exist as defined APIs, and are expected to be wrapped with a system portability layer. Maintaining core implementations within a portability layer is expected to require relatively few changes. This expedites any effort that may be necessary to run the :term:`NVDLA` software-stack on multiple platforms. With the appropriate portability layers in place, the same core implementations should compile as readily on both Linux and FreeRTOS. Similarly, on “headed” implementations that have a microcontroller closely coupled to :term:`NVDLA`, the existence of the portability layer makes it possible to run the same kernel mode driver on that microcontroller as would have run on the main CPU in a “headless” implementation that had no such companion microcontroller.

pred_answer: IRuntime 提交 inference job 的大致流程是：创建 runtime 实例，获取设备信息，加载网络，获取 tensor 信息，更新 tensors，分配内存，绑定 tensor，提交 task，最后卸载网络。（repo_sources/nvdla/doc/doc/sw/runtime_environment.rst:52-63）

evidence.statement: Documentation lists create runtime, device info, load network, tensor info/update, allocate/bind memory, submit, unload steps.
evidence.text:
Submitting task for inference using runtime interface includes below steps

#. :ref:`create_runtime_instance`
#. :ref:`device_information`
#. :ref:`load_network`
#. :ref:`tensor_information`
#. :ref:`update_tensors`
#. :ref:`allocate_memory`
#. :ref:`bind_tensor`
#. :ref:`submit_task`
#. :ref:`unload_network`

pred_answer: NVDLA compilation 是两步：Parsing 和 Compiling（repo_sources/nvdla/doc/doc/sw/compilation_tool.rst:7-8）。Parser 读取预训练模型（基本情况下是 Caffe 模型），把网络解析成适合下一步编译的层表示，并能提前发现不适合 NVDLA 实现的操作、拓扑错误或损坏数据（repo_sources/nvdla/doc/doc/sw/compilation_tool.rst:17-25）。Compiler 以 Parser 输出为输入，结合 NVDLA 硬件规格把网络操作映射成硬件层，生成适合可用硬件特性的操作、处理内存需求和量化，最终输出 NVDLA Loadable（repo_sources/nvdla/doc/doc/sw/compilation_tool.rst:17-25）。

evidence.statement: Compilation is a two-step process: Parsing and Compiling.
evidence.text: The Compilation phase is responsible for converting (aka compiling) a deep neural network into a sequence of hardware layers that are optimized for a given NVDLA configuration. Having a compiled network optimized for specific hardware configuration improves performance by reducing model size, load and run times. Compilation is a 2-step process that consists of: Parsing and Compiling.

evidence.statement: Parser reads model and produces network representation; Compiler maps operations to hardware layers and outputs NVDLA Loadable.
evidence.text:
Parser is a relatively simple tool to parse pre-trained models. In its most basic incarnation, it reads a pre-trained Caffe model and creates a suitable representation of the network as a set of layers and pass it down to the next step of Compilation. An advanced parser would allow translating pre-trained models on other frameworks like TensorFlow, Theano, etc into the same standard representation suitable for the compiler. Although the Compiler is the one who fully understands the underlying hardware configuration, the Parser is smart enough to pro-actively recognize those neural network operations that are not suitable for the NVDLA implmentation and report back to the application. Parser can also detect malformed networks with breaks/loops in the topology or corrupt trained data whose layout doesn't fit well for the associated network operation.

--------
Compiler
--------

Compiler takes the Parser's output as its input, and converts (i.e. compiles) it into a set of hardware layers using the understanding of underlying hardware specification for NVDLA implementation. The Compiler can interpret the parsed network as a directed graph with nodes representing the network operations and the edges representing the intermediate tensors between the operations. The main job of the Compiler is to effectively map each network operation onto the targetted functional block of the NVDLA implementation, keeping in mind the specifications of the hardware. Knowing the hardware configuration allows the Compiler to generate appropriate operations for the features that are available. For example, this might include selecting between different convolution operation modes (such as Winograd convolution, or Basic convolution), or splitting certain operations into multiple smaller units depending on the available hardware buffer sizes, etc. Optimizations or features in compiler can be controlled through input parameters to compiler.

In order to effectively execute the hardware layer, the Compiler is responsible to determine memory requirements for that layer. The choice of memory management can be flexible to the software implementation choice. This phase is also responsible for quantizing higher precision trained models (like 32-bit floating point) into lower precisions, such as 8-bit or 16-bit integer, or 16-bit floating point, and for determining memory allocations for the quantized weights during inferencing jobs. The output of the compiler is stored in a standardized file format called the :term:`NVDLA Loadable`.

evidence.text:
Compiler takes the Parser's output as its input, and converts (i.e. compiles) it into a set of hardware layers using the understanding of underlying hardware specification for NVDLA implementation. The Compiler can interpret the parsed network as a directed graph with nodes representing the network operations and the edges representing the intermediate tensors between the operations. The main job of the Compiler is to effectively map each network operation onto the targetted functional block of the NVDLA implementation, keeping in mind the specifications of the hardware. Knowing the hardware configuration allows the Compiler to generate appropriate operations for the features that are available. For example, this might include selecting between different convolution operation modes (such as Winograd convolution, or Basic convolution), or splitting certain operations into multiple smaller units depending on the available hardware buffer sizes, etc. Optimizations or features in compiler can be controlled through input parameters to compiler.

In order to effectively execute the hardware layer, the Compiler is responsible to determine memory requirements for that layer. The choice of memory management can be flexible to the software implementation choice. This phase is also responsible for quantizing higher precision trained models (like 32-bit floating point) into lower precisions, such as 8-bit or 16-bit integer, or 16-bit floating point, and for determining memory allocations for the quantized weights during inferencing jobs. The output of the compiler is stored in a standardized file format called the :term:`NVDLA Loadable`.

The same Compiler tool can be used to generate a list of hardware layers for multiple different NVDLA configurations. These steps are performed offline and might also be performed on the device that contains the NVDLA implementation.

pred_answer: NVDLA 的循环是：管理处理器先下发一个硬件层的配置和 activate 命令；硬件引擎执行 active task，完成后向管理处理器发 interrupt；管理处理器收到完成中断后再次启动同样流程。该 command-execute-interrupt flow 会一直重复，直到整个网络 inference 完成。若数据依赖允许，可并行激活多个 layer；每个 block 还有 double-buffer，可预取第二个 layer 的配置以便 active layer 完成后立即继续处理（repo_sources/nvdla/doc/doc/hw/v1/hwarch.rst:14-18）。

evidence.statement: Architecture doc describes processor sending config/activate, engines interrupting completion, and repeating until inference complete.
evidence.text:
Functional Description
=======================

NVDLA operation begins with the management processor (either a microcontroller or the main CPU) sending down the configuration of one hardware layer, along with an “activate” command. If data dependencies do not preclude this, multiple hardware layers can be sent down to different blocks and activated at the same time (i.e., if there exists another layer whose inputs do not depend on the output from the previous layer). Because every block has a double-buffer for its configuration registers, it can also capture a second layer’s configuration to begin immediately processing when the active layer has completed. Once a hardware engine finishes its active task, it will issue an interrupt to the management processor to report the completion, and the management processor will then begin the process again. This command-execute-interrupt flow repeats until inference on the entire network is complete.

pred_answer: Independent mode 是各个 block 单独配置执行时机和任务，每个 block 从内存到内存完成自己的操作，输入/输出都经过主系统内存或专用 SRAM。Fused mode 则把部分 block 组成流水线，绕过中间的内存往返，通过小 FIFO 直接传递数据；文档给的例子是 convolution core -> SDP -> PDP -> CDP，中间不做 memory-to-memory 操作。见 repo_sources/nvdla/doc/doc/hw/v1/hwarch.rst:19-20。

evidence.statement: Architecture doc contrasts memory-to-memory independent mode with pipelined fused mode through FIFOs.
evidence.text: NVDLA has two modes of operation: independent mode and fused mode. Independent operation refers to each individual block being configured for when and what it executes, with each block working on its assigned task. Independent operation begins and ends with the assigned block performing memory-to-memory operations, in and out of main system memory or dedicated SRAM memory. Fused operation is similar to independent operation, however, some blocks can be assembled as a pipeline; this improves performance by bypassing the round trip through memory, instead having blocks communicate with each other through small FIFOs (i.e., the convolution core can pass data to the Single Data Point Processor, which can pass data to the Planar Data Processor, and in turn to the Cross-channel Data Processor without performing memory-to-memory operations in between).

pred_answer: dla_interface.h 中 DLA_OP_* 一共列了 6 个 processor：BDMA、CONV、SDP、PDP、CDP、RUBIK（repo_sources/nvdla/sw/kmd/firmware/include/dla_interface.h:34-48）。

evidence.statement: Header defines DLA_OP_BDMA, CONV, SDP, PDP, CDP, RUBIK.
evidence.text:
/**
 * @ingroup Processors
 * @name DLA Processors
 * Processor modules in DLA engine. Each processor has it's
 * own operation a.k.a. HW layer. Network is formed using
 * graph of these operations
 * @{
 */
#define DLA_OP_BDMA		0
#define DLA_OP_CONV		1
#define DLA_OP_SDP		2
#define DLA_OP_PDP		3
#define DLA_OP_CDP		4
#define DLA_OP_RUBIK		5
/** @} */

pred_answer: 没有。BDMA “does not really have shadow copies for groups”；如果另一个 group 已经被 programmed 但还未 active，继续编程会落到同一个 group，所以需要等待另一个 group enabled。`dla_bdma_is_ready` 在检测到 `group_status` 中 next_group 的 bit 已置位且 `!next_group->active` 时返回 0。（repo_sources/nvdla/sw/kmd/firmware/bdma.c:205-224）

evidence.statement: Comment says BDMA does not really have shadow copies; returns 0 if another group is programmed but not active.

evidence.statement: Code checks dynamic ROI input layer, reads ROI descriptor from roi_array_addr.
Code computes address from surface_addr plus top*line_stride plus bpp*left.

pred_answer: 在 `nvdla_submit` ioctl 路径中，用户传入的 `args->tasks` 先被转成 `user_task` 指针，并通过 `copy_from_user` 拷贝到内核侧 `local_task`；随后驱动分配 `struct nvdla_task`，设置 `nvdla_dev->task`、引用计数、设备和文件上下文，再用 `nvdla_fill_task_desc(&local_task, task)` 把用户 task 描述填进内核 task，最后调用 `nvdla_task_submit(nvdla_dev, task)` 进入后续提交路径。证据只展示到这里，未展示 `nvdla_task_submit` 内部如何把 task 交给 firmware。（repo_sources/nvdla/sw/kmd/port/linux/nvdla_gem.c:79-115）

evidence.statement: nvdla_submit copies user task, allocates task, fills task desc, and calls nvdla_task_submit.

pred_answer: 不是。`nvdla_fill_task_desc` 先把 `local_task->num_addresses` 记录到 `task->num_addresses`，然后按这个数量分配 `struct nvdla_mem_handle` 数组，并用 `copy_from_user` 从用户态的 `local_task->address_list` 拷贝到该数组；失败会释放并返回 `-EFAULT`，成功后把 `task->address_list` 指向这块内核分配的 `handles`（repo_sources/nvdla/sw/kmd/port/linux/nvdla_gem.c:51-76）。

evidence.statement: nvdla_fill_task_desc copies num_addresses and copies address_list from user into allocated handles.

evidence.statement: dla_get_dma_address dispatches DESTINATION_PROCESSOR to dla_read_cpu_address and DESTINATION_DMA to dla_read_dma_address.

pred_answer: 是的，Linux callbacks 里的 `dla_data_write` 和 `dla_data_read` 都是先 `dma_buf_get`，再 `dma_buf_begin_cpu_access(..., DMA_BIDIRECTIONAL)`，然后 `dma_buf_vmap`，用 `memcpy` 访问映射地址加 `offset`，最后 `dma_buf_vunmap`/结束 CPU access/put buffer。差别在拷贝方向和句柄索引：`dla_data_write` 用 `handles[dst].handle`，把 `src` 拷到 `(uint8_t *)ptr + offset`；`dla_data_read` 用 `handles[src].handle`，把 `(uint8_t *)ptr + offset` 拷到 `dst`。见 `repo_sources/nvdla/sw/kmd/port/linux/nvdla_core_callbacks.c:222-264` 和 `repo_sources/nvdla/sw/kmd/port/linux/nvdla_core_callbacks.c:266-308`。

evidence.statement: dla_data_write gets dma_buf, begins CPU access, vmaps, memcpy from src into mapped buffer plus offset, then unmaps.
dla_data_read gets dma_buf, begins CPU access, vmaps, memcpy from mapped buffer plus offset into dst, then unmaps.

pred_answer: 有。`Runtime::load` 读取 loadable 的 submit/task/memory entries 后，检查 `m_submit_entries.size() < 1 || m_task_entries.size() < 1 || m_memory_entries.size() < 1`；任一少于 1 都会报错并加载失败，因此 submit、task、memory 都至少需要 1 个 entry（repo_sources/nvdla/sw/umd/core/src/runtime/Runtime.cpp:287-304）。

evidence.statement: Runtime::bindInputTensor checks index, finds memories whose inputBindId equals index, and sets handle/virt addr.

pred_answer: Runtime::fillTaskAddressList 先检查 task 的 address_list 条目数不能超过 NVDLA_MAX_BUFFERS_PER_TASK，超出就报错并返回 false；随后对每个条目检查 address id 非负且小于 m_address.size()，再检查对应 memory_id 非负且小于 m_memory.size()，最后检查 m_memory[memory_id].hMem 不是 0，任一失败都返回 false（repo_sources/nvdla/sw/umd/core/src/runtime/Runtime.cpp:481-516）。只有这些校验通过后，才把 handle 和 offset 写入 dla_task->address_list[ali]（repo_sources/nvdla/sw/umd/core/src/runtime/Runtime.cpp:518-523）。

evidence.statement: Runtime::fillTaskAddressList checks max buffers, address id bounds, memory id bounds, and null hMem.
After validation, it writes handle and offset into dla_task address_list.

  ,bdma2csb_resp_valid           //|> o
  ,bdma2cvif_rd_cdt_lat_fifo_pop //|> o

  ,bdma2cvif_rd_req_valid        //|> o

  ,bdma2mcif_wr_req_pd           //|> o
  ,bdma2mcif_wr_req_valid        //|> o

//
// NV_NVDLA_bdma_ports.v
//
input  nvdla_core_clk;   /* bdma2csb_resp, bdma2cvif_rd_cdt, bdma2cvif_rd_req, bdma2cvif_wr_req, bdma2glb_done_intr, bdma2mcif_rd_cdt, bdma2mcif_rd_req, bdma2mcif_wr_req, csb2bdma_req, cvif2bdma_rd_rsp, cvif2bdma_wr_rsp, mcif2bdma_rd_rsp, mcif2bdma_wr_rsp */
input  nvdla_core_rstn;  /* bdma2csb_resp, bdma2cvif_rd_cdt, bdma2cvif_rd_req, bdma2cvif_wr_req, bdma2glb_done_intr, bdma2mcif_rd_cdt, bdma2mcif_rd_req, bdma2mcif_wr_req, csb2bdma_req, cvif2bdma_rd_rsp, cvif2bdma_wr_rsp, mcif2bdma_rd_rsp, mcif2bdma_wr_rsp */

output        bdma2csb_resp_valid;  /* data valid */
output [33:0] bdma2csb_resp_pd;     /* pkt_id_width=1 pkt_widths=33,33  */

output  bdma2cvif_rd_cdt_lat_fifo_pop;

output        bdma2cvif_rd_req_valid;  /* data valid */
input         bdma2cvif_rd_req_ready;  /* data return handshake */
output [78:0] bdma2cvif_rd_req_pd;

output         bdma2cvif_wr_req_valid;  /* data valid */
input          bdma2cvif_wr_req_ready;  /* data return handshake */
output [514:0] bdma2cvif_wr_req_pd;     /* pkt_id_width=1 pkt_widths=78,514  */

output [1:0] bdma2glb_done_intr_pd;

output  bdma2mcif_rd_cdt_lat_fifo_pop;

output        bdma2mcif_rd_req_valid;  /* data valid */
input         bdma2mcif_rd_req_ready;  /* data return handshake */
output [78:0] bdma2mcif_rd_req_pd;

output         bdma2mcif_wr_req_valid;  /* data valid */
input          bdma2mcif_wr_req_ready;  /* data return handshake */
output [514:0] bdma2mcif_wr_req_pd;     /* pkt_id_width=1 pkt_widths=78,514  */

input         csb2bdma_req_pvld;  /* data valid */
output        csb2bdma_req_prdy;  /* data return handshake */
